title: acaena ovalifolia subsp. elegans
intro: Acaena ovalifolia subsp. elegans, also known as the silver button or New Zealand silver fern, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its silver-gray foliage and small white flowers. The leaves of Acaena ovalifolia subsp. elegans have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular remedy for treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is also known to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Acaena ovalifolia subsp. elegans can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
